name: environment_sensor_fusion_upgrade_redwood_car_fair
premise: A Environment field incident unit is in a fast-moving incident involving environment sensor fusion upgrade redwood car fair. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Every delay increases ecological harm, community impact, and compliance. Stakeholders are already framing this as care vs fairness under pressure. A whistleblower has already contacted media; the narrative is forming now. Constraints: Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Luc Kaur
agents.identity: I am Luc Kaur, accountable for a defensible call in Environment when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays fairness for care.

agents.name: Elliot Vasquez
agents.identity: I am Elliot Vasquez, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Environment.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so fairness isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of care.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect fairness.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to fairness to secure care under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Evidence is partial. Luc Kaur must decide under care vs fairness. What does Luc Kaur do?
